summary.intent: Address a second round of Codex review findings on PR #669 in the ars-ui repo, specifically hardening edge cases in the combobox component around composition end guarding, controlled prop sync, and hidden input serialization.
summary.outcome: All four new Codex review threads were addressed with failing regression tests written first, then fixed implementations committed as 'fix(components): harden combobox review edge cases'. The commit was pushed and all four threads were replied to and resolved.

repo: The project uses an iterative Codex review loop: post '@codex review', wait for eyes reaction as active-review signal, poll for unresolved threads on eyes drop, fix findings, push, reply+resolve threads, then re-trigger.

The combobox component (crates/ars-components/src/selection/combobox/mod.rs) has nuanced state management including TransitionPlan, composition state, controlled vs uncontrolled prop sync, and hidden input serialization with sentinel/delimiter encoding.
Set::All uses a stable sentinel in hidden input serialization that must not be broken by key escaping logic.
CompositionEnd event handling requires readonly/disabled guards before mutating state.
Controlled input prop sync needs to recompute open state (close on zero results) when external props change.

code.finding: CompositionEnd handler - needed readonly/disabled guard before clearing composition state
Hidden input serialization - needed key escaping to avoid sentinel/delimiter collisions
Prop sync / controlled input path - needed open-state recomputation on zero results
TransitionPlan context_only/state fields examined during Codex thread investigation

workflow: TDD loop: write failing regression tests targeting the exact Codex findings before touching implementation, then fix until green.
cargo fmt --check should be run before committing; the session caught a formatting drift after applying patches.
Verification sequence used: cargo fmt --check → cargo test (full suite) → cargo clippy -D warnings → cargo llvm-cov (targeted coverage).
Codex review polling cadence: 120-second sleep intervals while eyes reaction is present; 15-minute threshold before treating the run as possibly stalled.
Commit message convention includes 'Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>' when addressing Codex review findings.
A composition test assertion needed to be loosened: asserting that composition end clears composition state is the important contract, not that pre-existing filtered state is exactly None.

friction: Long Codex review wait: multiple 120-second polling cycles were needed, spanning well past the 15-minute stall threshold before eyes finally dropped.
One regression test over-specified an internal state invariant (filtered state must be None), requiring a test adjustment after the implementation fix was applied.
cargo fmt --check caught a formatting issue only after patches were applied, requiring an extra fmt + recheck cycle.
CI jobs (clippy, workspace check, unit tests) were slow to progress, with several polls showing pending status on those gates.
open_items: The Codex review loop is iterative — a third '@codex review' was posted at the end; any new findings from that pass would need a subsequent fix cycle.
Coverage numbers from cargo llvm-cov were not explicitly recorded; no baseline comparison was made to confirm coverage improved or held steady.
The 15-minute Codex stall threshold logic was referenced but never triggered in this session — the behavior when it is triggered should be validated.
